solutions.solution:
All the sentences are actual information except the first. Out of these six second and the fifth sentences give information on parent-child relationships. We can begin with either of the two. Suppose we begin with the sixth sentence. The diagram is:

[Diagram showing A at top, with D below A]

Since we do not want to make many diagrams and instead we want to prefer to only add to the existing diagrams, therefore, we should look for sentences that talk of A or D. The third sentence talks about A. Hence, we add this information, that A and C are married couple, in our diagram.

The first sentence is U.S.I. while all others are actual information. We start with the 3rd sentence since it gives a parent-child relationship. The diagram is:

[Diagram showing A at top with C below, and notation that D is the only son of C who is the brother of A.]
